slots.2_caller_identity: I review wildlife removal sites for a living
slots.3_target_buyer: Owner-operator or manager at residential wildlife removal company (raccoons, squirrels, bats, skunks, opossums, birds)
slots.5_dollar_anchor: $2,000 to $6,000 a week in missed jobs
slots.5_anchor_math: 5-10 missed bookings/wk × $400-$600 avg wildlife removal job = $2K-$6K/wk for smaller operators (1-2 trucks). Avg job data: raccoon $200-$600, squirrel $300-$600, bat $250-$600, attic jobs $500-$1,500 (HomeGuide 2026, Angi 2026). Emergency wildlife = high-intent, caller hires first shop that picks up. At 50% form-completion rate from market_cpc.json baseline (2%), 5-10 missed jobs/wk is conservative for any operator running paid ads.
slots.6_cpc_range: $8.50 to $26.80 per click depending on metro
slots.6_cpc_source: market_cpc.json — Q2 2026 estimates. National avg $8.50-$16.00; NYC top $26.80; Austin bat removal $11.20-$22.00.

7_issues_hierarchy.plain_english: The top of your page — no clear phone number, no urgency
7_issues_hierarchy.frequency: 53 occurrences across 102 prospects (top leak by count)
7_issues_hierarchy.note: Emergency-service buyer on mobile — if the number isn't right at the top, they're gone in 3 seconds

7_issues_hierarchy.plain_english: How they actually reach you — form before they've decided anything, or no email at all
7_issues_hierarchy.frequency: 21 no email + 4 weak booking / CTA = 25 occurrences
7_issues_hierarchy.note: Asking for info before the buyer commits creates a wall — they open two more tabs

7_issues_hierarchy.issue: Trust gap — no credentials, no reviews, no specialist signal
7_issues_hierarchy.plain_english: TEASED — revealed only in the audit
7_issues_hierarchy.frequency: Hidden trust signals + SMS-only (6) + Gmail/unprofessional email (3) + no specialist identity (5) = ~14 occurrences
7_issues_hierarchy.note: The invisible third issue — this is the audit reveal. Do not name it on the call.
Kills desktop and B2B/HOA buyer conversions

7_issues_hierarchy.plain_english: Wildlife removal buried as a sub-service on a general pest site
7_issues_hierarchy.note: Homeowner searching wildlife removal doesn't trust a general pest brand as much as a specialist

9_banned_terms: Miner-style apology hedges (appreciate you picking up, out of the blue, calling cold)
Slang permission (got a sec, cool?, Quick on me, 90 seconds)
CTA (use 'booking button' / 'how they book' / 'how they actually reach you')
hero / hero copy (use 'top of your page' / 'first thing they see')
above the fold (use 'top of the page')
conversion rate (use 'how many actually book' / 'how many actually call')
funnel (use 'booking process' / 'the steps to reach you')
TTI / LCP / Core Web Vitals / page speed (use 'how fast it loads')
developer / dev work / dev (frame fixes as quick wins, not dev tasks)
Quick context — BANNED (use 'Here's the deal' / 'Here's what's going on' / 'Here's what I did')
competitor names by name — use 'the next shop' / 'the next ad' / 'the next listing'
pest control (this is wildlife removal only — do not conflate)
10_three_pillars: Booking friction (how they actually reach you)

11_cta_breakdown.note: ~50% of prospects have weak or moderate booking actions — significant opportunity across the board

universal_voice_locks: Plain English — no acronyms, no CRO jargon, no dev language
No {company} in beat copy — use 'your site'
Dual-path close (booked call OR email sample)
'Quick context' BANNED — use 'Here's the deal' / 'Here's what's going on' / 'Here's what I did'
No competitor names — use 'the next shop' / 'the next ad'

verbatim_site_language.audit_format: Thirty minutes. Your live site on screen.
verbatim_site_language.plain_english_differentiator: Every leak named in plain English no agency would dare use.
verbatim_site_language.just_numbers: Just the numbers, in your numbers.
verbatim_site_language.yours_to_keep: Yours to keep whether you hire me to ship it or not.
verbatim_site_language.sequenced_plan: A sequenced fix-list ranked by conversion impact and effort to ship.
verbatim_site_language.tiered_wins: Quick wins first — quick wins, medium lifts, full rebuilds.
verbatim_site_language.emergency_frame: Emergency-service buying — caller hires the first shop that makes it easy.
verbatim_site_language.midnight_hook: Homeowner hears scratching at night, taps your ad, can't find your number, books the next shop.

A.name: The Missed-Call Leak
A.hook: Your booking process is costing you $2K-$6K/week — homeowner panics, taps your ad, can't book fast enough, calls the next shop
A.data_backing: 53/102 prospects have no/buried phone — top CRO leak by count
A.lever: Dollar anchor + urgency of emergency-service mindset
A.best_for: Operators running paid ads where every click costs $8-$22

B.name: Midnight Emergency Steal
B.hook: Homeowner hears scratching at 11pm. Taps your ad. Your phone isn't front and center. Taps the next shop. Next shop gets the $600 job by 9am.
B.data_backing: Phone burial + no contact = 53 + 21 occurrences; emergency-service buyer behavior from call_intel.json
B.lever: Status loss + FOMO + competitor steal frame (no names)
B.best_for: Operators in competitive markets where response speed is the differentiator

C.name: 3-Issue Reveal
C.hook: I pulled up your site and saw three issues — the worst one is invisible to most operators
C.data_backing: Top of page (53 phone leaks) + booking friction (25 form/email leaks) + trust gap (teased)
C.lever: Curiosity gap + loss aversion — hold the worst issue back for the audit
C.best_for: Operators with 'we just updated the site' objection-prone responses

beat_skeleton.2_PROBLEM: 17-24s — emergency-service context, ends with question they can't answer
beat_skeleton.3_AGITATION: 30-40s — concrete walk-through + dollar anchor + steal frame
beat_skeleton.5_AUDIT_OFFER: 33s — $1,500 audit, free, 30 min shared screen, dollar-anchored per leak
beat_skeleton.6_CLOSE: 30s — Thursday 10 or Friday 2, OR sample audit by email

industry_context.vertical: Residential wildlife removal — raccoons, squirrels, bats, skunks, opossums, birds, iguanas, rats
industry_context.NOT: General pest control — this is wildlife sub-vertical only
industry_context.buyer_mindset: Emergency service — homeowner is panicking, will hire first shop that picks up or makes it easy to book
industry_context.mobile_behavior: Emergency searches happen at night on personal cell — 3-second decision window
